meta.name: PGSI Problem Gambling Severity Index
meta.description: The PGSI is the core component of the Canadian Problem Gambling Index (CPGI), consisting of 9 items to assess the severity of gambling problems. Suitable for gambling risk screening in the general population.
meta.author: Ferris & Wynne (2001)
meta.reference: Ferris J, Wynne H. The Canadian Problem Gambling Index: Final report. Ottawa: Canadian Centre on Substance Abuse; 2001.
meta.instruction: Thinking about the last 12 months, how often have the following situations applied to you?

questions.text: Have you bet more than you could really afford to lose
Have you needed to gamble with larger amounts of money to get the same feeling of excitement
When you gambled, did you go back another day to try to win back the money you lost
Have you borrowed money or sold anything to get money to gamble
Have you felt that you might have a problem with gambling
Has gambling caused you any health problems, including stress or anxiety
Have people criticized your betting or told you that you had a gambling problem, regardless of whether or not you thought it was true
Has your gambling caused any financial problems for you or your household
Have you felt guilty about the way you gamble or what happens when you gamble

ranges.label: Non-Problem Gambling
ranges.description: You did not report any signs of problem gambling. This indicates that your current substance-use-related risk is low and within a healthy range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy recreational habits
If you do gamble, set time and money limits
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Stay informed about risks related to substance use
Maintain the ability to make autonomous choices in social situations

ranges.label: Low Risk Gambling
ranges.description: You reported minor gambling-related issues at a low risk level. A mild risk level suggests it may be helpful to pay attention to your usage patterns and maintain self-awareness.
suggestions: Maintain self-awareness about gambling behavior
Set clear limits on gambling time and money
Don't use gambling to cope with stress or emotional issues
Pay attention to your usage patterns and frequency
Learn about recommended safe-use limits

ranges.label: Moderate Risk Gambling
ranges.description: Your gambling behavior may be causing some negative consequences. Consider taking action. Your score suggests that substance use may already be affecting certain areas of your life. Further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Seriously examine how gambling affects your life
Set strict gambling budgets and stick to them
Consider seeking professional help or calling a gambling helpline
Learn about risk factors for problem gambling
Consider consulting an addiction medicine professional

ranges.label: Problem Gambling
ranges.description: Your gambling behavior has caused significant negative consequences, meeting criteria for problem gambling. Professional help is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommend seeking professional gambling treatment
Let trusted family or friends know about your situation
Consider self-exclusion programs (banning yourself from gambling venues)
Address underlying emotional or mental health issues
Join a support group (e.g., Gamblers Anonymous)
